T0518.definition: An electronic document issued by a trusted third party (Certificate Authority, CA) to verify the identity of a public key holder.
T0518.description: A digital certificate is a core component of the Public Key Infrastructure (PKI), formatted according to the X.509 standard. It contains the public key, holder information, issuer details, validity period, and a digital signature. SSL/TLS certificates are used for identity authentication and encrypted communication on HTTPS websites, while code signing certificates are used to verify the identity of software publishers.
T0518.usageExample: After deploying an SSL certificate on a website, a lock icon appears in the browser's address bar, allowing users to confirm the website's identity and communicate securely.

references.title: RFC 5280: Internet X.509 Public Key Infrastructure Certificate
